used_quotes: A good briefing tells you what happened. A great one tells you what people are arguing about.
Ah, but a man's reach should exceed his grasp, / Or what's a heaven for?
All things are an exchange for fire, and fire for all things, as goods for gold and gold for goods.
Better is the end of a thing than the beginning thereof: and the patient in spirit is better than the proud in spirit.
Every day is a journey, and the journey itself is home.
How did you go bankrupt?… Gradually and then suddenly.
How we spend our days is, of course, how we spend our lives.
I turn the wheel that spins; I delight to see the high come down and the low ascend.
In the fields of observation, chance favours only the prepared mind.
Insist on yourself; never imitate.
It is the mark of an educated mind to be able to entertain a thought without accepting it.
Memory is the treasury and guardian of all things.
Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood.
Peace cannot be kept by force; it can only be achieved by understanding.
Peace hath her victories, no less renown'd than war.
Somewhere, something incredible is waiting to be known.
The best way out is always through.
The condition upon which God hath given liberty to man is eternal vigilance.
The cost of a thing is the amount of what I will call life which is required to be exchanged for it, immediately or in the long run.
The first principle is that you must not fool yourself — and you are the easiest person to fool.
The future is already here — it's just not very evenly distributed.
The map is not the territory.
The old order changeth, yielding place to new, / And God fulfils himself in many ways, / Lest one good custom should corrupt the world.
The owl of Minerva spreads its wings only with the falling of the dusk.
The real voyage of discovery consists not in seeking new landscapes, but in having new eyes.
There are more things likely to frighten us than there are to crush us; we suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
There is a tide in the affairs of men, which, taken at the flood, leads on to fortune; omitted, all the voyage of their life is bound in shallows and in miseries.
Thirty spokes share the wheel's hub; it is the centre hole that makes it useful. … Therefore profit comes from what is there; usefulness from what is not there.
To travel hopefully is a better thing than to arrive, and the true success is to labour.
Two things fill the mind with ever new and increasing admiration and awe, the more often and steadily we reflect upon them: the starry heavens above me and the moral law within me.
We shape our buildings; thereafter they shape us.
Well done is better than well said.
When we try to pick out anything by itself, we find it hitched to everything else in the Universe.
When you can measure what you are speaking about, and express it in numbers, you know something about it … but when you cannot measure it … your knowledge is of a meagre and unsatisfactory kind.
Where is the wisdom we have lost in knowledge? Where is the knowledge we have lost in information?
Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.
“Though the mills of God grind slowly, yet they grind exceeding small; / Though with patience He stands waiting, with exactness grinds He all.”
